metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A American city where Basilica of the First Accord, Beacon Citadel, Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Aquila Matara should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Aquila Matara organizes power through Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel, public response through Beacon Citadel, civic memory through Basilica of the First Accord, and daily life through S1 Public Bathhouse, S2 Grand Market, S3 University, S4 Guild Hall, S5 Forum.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Polished White Limestone, Red Brick, Pale Marble Stone, Gold Trim, Red Fabric, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Record Wards, and exact anchors including Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel, Beacon Citadel, Basilica of the First Accord, Hall of Archives, Great Clock Tower, Grand Cathedral, Public Bathhouse, Wall of Oaths, Lantern Square, Grand Market.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A American civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Basilica of the First Accord, Beacon Citadel, Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel, and section anchors such as Public Bathhouse, Grand Market, University, Guild Hall, Forum.

canonLocks: Aquila Matara uses city code AQM.
The Wonder is Basilica of the First Accord.
The Beacon is Beacon Citadel.
The Seat of Power is Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.
The source-backed section spine is: S1 Public Bathhouse, S2 Grand Market, S3 University, S4 Guild Hall, S5 Forum.
Preserve exact anchor name: Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel.
Preserve exact anchor name: Beacon Citadel.
Preserve exact anchor name: Basilica of the First Accord.
Preserve exact anchor name: Hall of Archives.
Preserve exact anchor name: Great Clock Tower.
Preserve exact anchor name: Grand Cathedral.
Preserve exact anchor name: Public Bathhouse.
Preserve exact anchor name: Wall of Oaths.
Preserve exact anchor name: Lantern Square.
Preserve exact anchor name: Grand Market.
Preserve exact anchor name: Founders Obelisk.
Preserve exact anchor name: Pilgrim's Well.
Preserve exact anchor name: University.
Preserve exact anchor name: Royal Observatory.
Preserve exact anchor name: Eternal Watch Flame.
Preserve exact anchor name: Founders Bridge.
Preserve exact anchor name: Guild Hall.
Preserve exact anchor name: Monument of the Citizen.
Preserve exact anchor name: Three Sisters Fountain.
Preserve exact anchor name: Forum.
Preserve exact anchor name: Pillar of the First Accord.
Preserve exact anchor name: Old Bell Tower.
rejectedReads: Do not treat Aquila Matara as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Aquila Matara's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

infrastructure: records, permits, markets, services, or rituals implied by anchors
colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as American and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as Basilica of the First Accord, Wall of Oaths.
customs: citizens orient public life around Public Bathhouse in Section 1
citizens orient public life around Grand Market in Section 2
citizens orient public life around University in Section 3
citizens orient public life around Guild Hall in Section 4
citizens orient public life around Forum in Section 5
beacon changes become public behavior, not background color
exact landmark names carry social memory and directions
civic errands happen visibly at thresholds, queues, ledgers, stalls, courts, or shrines
bodyLanguage: workers move with practiced familiarity around district anchors
officials use thresholds and raised places to control attention
residents treat named landmarks as everyday orientation points
speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors
local speech distinguishes civic authority, ordinary errands, and landmark obligations
people refer to D13 as the seat of power rather than a faction headquarters

streets.appearance: Streets should show Aquila Matara's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Public Bathhouse, S2 Grand Market, S3 University, S4 Guild Hall, S5 Forum. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.

districts.name: D01 — Public Bathhouse Civic-Anchor Precinct
districts.civicPurpose: This district turns Section 1's source-backed anchor set into a board-scale civic space: Public Bathhouse, Beacon Citadel, Wall of Oaths, Lantern Square. It supports scenes of public movement, ordinary business, local obligation, and visible pressure within Aquila Matara.
districts.visualIdentity: Use Public Bathhouse as the main read. Keep exact source anchors visible where applicable: Public Bathhouse, Beacon Citadel, Wall of Oaths, Lantern Square. The district should share Aquila Matara's American identity, Polished White Limestone, Red Brick, Pale Marble Stone, Gold Trim, and Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin without becoming generic.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Locally grounded, with Aquila Matara's larger mood shaped by Basilica of the First Accord, Beacon Citadel, and Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Aquila Matara civilians using the district
clear roads, thresholds, or plaza edges for tactical readability
narrativeUse: scenes centered on Public Bathhouse
public errands, waiting, negotiation, or conflict
showing how civic systems shape ordinary movement
a district-level visual establishing shot for prose or diorama use
local color that still advances plot, logistics, or social pressure

seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. Built for Aquila Matara's American population, it uses Polished White Limestone, Red Brick, Pale Marble Stone, Gold Trim, Red Fabric across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast; magic expression: Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Record Wards. districtDioramaGuidance: Dioramas must support 25 district slots: S1 covers D01, D02, D03, D06, D07 and uses source-backed anchors Public Bathhouse, Beacon Citadel, Wall of Oaths, Lantern Square.
Dioramas must support 25 district slots: S2 covers D04, D05, D09, D10, D15 and uses source-backed anchors Grand Market, Great Clock Tower, Founders Obelisk, Pilgrim's Well.
Dioramas must support 25 district slots: S3 covers D08, D12, D13, D14, D18 and uses source-backed anchors University, Royal Observatory, Eternal Watch Flame, Founders Bridge.
Dioramas must support 25 district slots: S4 covers D11, D16, D17, D21, D22 and uses source-backed anchors Guild Hall, Grand Cathedral, Monument of the Citizen, Three Sisters Fountain.
Dioramas must support 25 district slots: S5 covers D19, D20, D23, D24, D25 and uses source-backed anchors Forum, Hall of Archives, Pillar of the First Accord, Old Bell Tower.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds must show Basilica of the First Accord as the wonder-scale identity anchor, Civic Blue Glow Tide-Seal Citadel at D13, and Beacon Citadel as the city signal landmark.
Preserve the five-section spine: S1 Public Bathhouse, S2 Grand Market, S3 University, S4 Guild Hall, S5 Forum.
Use roads, terrain, water, civic terraces, walls, markets, courts, or service routes only where they support the exact city identity and tactical readability.
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or research-log content in the JSON
generic fantasy architecture that ignores exact anchor names
readable real-world text labels baked into images
collapsing the 25-district board into only five playable spaces
making landmarks decorative when they should have civic, narrative, and asset function
